segments.text: This drone is smaller than a bird, but it is the Indian army's deadliest secret weapon. It was designed to solve a massive problem that normal military
drones simply cannot see. Regular drones are loud and [music] large. The moment army sends one in, enemies hear it
coming and they disappear. The mission fails before it even starts. So, they bought Black Hornet. It weighs just 18
g, fits in the palm of your hand, and its motor make absolutely no sound. It flies straight into enemy territory and
nobody even knows it's there. But what if enemies are hiding in a pitch black building where the GPS would not even work? This tiny drone uses built-in
thermal cameras to see body heat in the dark. And its smart design lets it navigate perfectly indoors without
needing any GPS signals. But wait, if it is that small and the enemy catches it,
won't they steal the army's recorded video data? Actually, no. While making it, they made sure it stores absolutely no data. Everything is sent straight to
the soldier screen in real time. If it's caught, the enemy gets nothing but plastic. Such an innovative design.
